It features a “news” section with press releases, a photo gallery, and a social media tab that brings together the White House’s presence across platforms. articles.title: New Aadhaar app, Sanchar Saathi to DigiLocker: 5 important government apps you need on your phone for daily convenience
articles.content: In today’s increasingly digital world, wherein everything is available online at the click of a button. Many people today don’t know about the useful and life-saving government apps, which often come in handy. These government apps are very convenient to use, and they make availing government services much easier. More importantly, these apps have streamlined services like Aadhaar-linked payments, certificate downloads, and grievance redressal; it empowers citizens with instant access to welfare schemes and official updates.

articles.title: Charge your EV for free this weekend at ACMobility chargers
articles.content: ACMobility will offer free charging across its EV charging network from March 27 to 29, 2026, allowing electric vehicle owners to use its public chargers without cost for three days. The offer is open to all EV brands, as long as the vehicle can connect to ACMobility chargers. Supported connectors include Type 2, GBT, and CCS2, although availability will depend on the charging hub. **media[89446]** All operational ACMobility charging sites listed on the Evro app are covered by the promo. Access will still follow the operating hours of each location, particularly for chargers located in malls and commercial areas. **media[89449]** Some locations remain restricted. Charging stations inside private properties such as residential developments or members-only facilities will not be open to the general public. Residents and members in those locations, however, can still use the chargers for free during the promo period. There is no limit on charging time or energy use per vehicle during the three-day run. ACMobility is asking users to stop charging at around 80 percent battery level to help improve charger availability for others. Charging slots will be available on a first-come, first-served basis. Users can check real-time availability through the Evro app, which is also required to start a charging session. Walk-ins are allowed, but access will depend on whether a charger is free at the time. The promo also covers plug-in capable vehicles, as long as they are compatible with ACMobility’s chargers. **media[89447]** Users must use the Evro app to locate compatible stations and begin charging sessions. While the promo covers all operational public ACMobility hubs, access to private or residents-only sites will remain limited to authorized users.

articles.title: Are CEOs being replaced by AI? Coca-Cola and Walmart top leaders exit, pointing to the growing impact of Artificial Intelligence
articles.content: Artificial intelligence is becoming more advanced with each passing day. It is changing how people work, as AI agents are being used by more and more people in their daily workflows. On the other hand, companies are laying off employees in entry-level positions after automating these positions. Not just small companies but big American companies like Google, Meta, Microsoft, and Amazon are also laying off people. Interestingly, until now, everyone was of the opinion that AI is going to first take away jobs of the entry-level workers.

articles.title: Dhurandhar 2 Characters Make Debut In Accounts Exam Questions; Paper Goes Viral
articles.content:
Dhurandhar: The Revenge, popularly known as Dhurandhar 2, has made its way into classrooms, though not in the way one might expect. The film isn’t being discussed in a debate or essay competition; instead, it has appeared on an accounts exam paper. A social media post showing a question paper inspired by characters from the film has now gone viral online.

Viral Accounts Exam Paper

The question paper features three problems, each using the names of characters played by the film’s star cast.

ALSO READ| Dhurandhar 2 Sees Drop On Second Friday; Earns Slightly Over Half Of First Friday

The first question reads: “Jameel Jamali, SP Chaudhary Aslam and Yalina Jamali share profits and losses of the firm equally. SP Chaudhary retires from business. New profit sharing ratio would be: a. 1:1; b. 2:3; c. 7:8 and d. 3:5.”

“Ajay Sanyal, Sushant Bansal are partners in a firm. They admitted Mohhamad Aalam as a new partner. Aalam Bought 1,00,000 as capital and 50,000 for Goodwill. Pass the necessary journal entry,” read the second question.

The third one, which features Hamza Ali Mazari, Rehman Dakait and Uzair Baloch, are partners in a firm sharing profits in the ratio 2: 2: 1 and the Balance Sheet of the firm. It asked the students to prepare the revaluation A/c, partners’ capital A/cs and Balance Sheet.

However, one interesting input here caught the attention of many. “Rehman Dakait died on December 5, 2025.”

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by Viral Bhayani (@viralbhayani)

How Did Social Media Users React To This?

While many dropped laughing emoticons in the comments section of the viral question paper, others attempted it.

ALSO READ| Dhurandhar 2 Box Office Collection: Ranveer Singh Film Crosses Rs 1100 Cr Mark Despite 56.7% Drop

“Accounting is always interesting,” said one social media user.

Another added, “Examiner is Dhurandhar paglu.”

“Superb!” commented a third.

A fourth posted, “Rehman Dakait died on 5th December 2025 the exact date when the movie was released, peak detailing by examiner.”
